title: 10 best herbal capsules for hangover
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for hangovers are dietary supplements designed to alleviate the symptoms of excessive alcohol consumption. They typically contain a blend of herbs, vitamins, and minerals that help detoxify the body, replenish lost nutrients, and support liver function. Examples of herbs commonly found in these capsules include milk thistle, ginger, prickly pear, and artichoke extract. By taking herbal capsules for hangovers, individuals can reduce the severity of symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue, enabling them to resume their daily activities with minimal disruption. For instance, a person who consumes alcohol at a social event can take these capsules before going to bed, wake up feeling refreshed, and avoid missing work or important obligations the next day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsule helps with hangover because it is rich in vitamins and antioxidants, particularly vitamin C and A, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption. The capsaicin in bell peppers can also help improve metabolism and speed up the breakdown and elimination of alcohol from the body. Additionally, bell peppers contain flavonoids and carotenoids that can help protect the liver from damage caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Overall, bell pepper capsules can help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue, and support overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper capsules contain high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation in the body, important for hangover as alcohol consumption can cause inflammation and irritation in the stomach and intestines.

Antioxidant: Bell peppers are rich in antioxidants, such as vitamin C and carotenoids, which help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, important for hangover as alcohol consumption can increase the production of free radicals in the body.

Diuretic: Bell pepper capsules contain vitamin C and other compounds that can help increase urine production, important for hangover as alcohol consumption can lead to dehydration and the accumulation of toxins in the body.

Nutrient-dense: Bell peppers are high in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, vitamin A, and potassium, which can help replenish nutrients lost due to alcohol consumption, important for hangover as alcohol can deplete the body of essential nutrients.

Alkalizing: Bell peppers have an alkalizing effect on the body, which can help balance the acidity caused by alcohol consumption, important for hangover as alcohol can disrupt the body's pH balance and lead to symptoms such as nausea and headaches.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Contain high levels of antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption.
Leaves: Rich in vitamins and minerals, which can help replenish essential nutrients lost during a hangover.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Stems: Contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have liver-protective effects and can help speed up alcohol metabolism.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsule helps with hangover because it includes active compounds that support liver function and help the body break down alcohol more efficiently. The capsules often contain extracts from the leaves of the maidenhair tree, which have antioxidant properties that can protect liver cells from damage caused by alcohol consumption. Additionally, the capsules may help replenish essential nutrients, such as vitamins and minerals, that are depleted during heavy drinking, further aiding in the recovery process. By promoting liver health and providing necessary nutrients, maidenhair tree capsules can help alleviate hangover symptoms like nausea, headache, and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of maidenhair tree capsules helps reduce inflammation in the body caused by alcohol consumption, which is important for hangover relief. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins in the plant.

Antioxidant: Maidenhair tree capsules have strong antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals in the body, which are produced during alcohol metabolism and can worsen hangover symptoms. The antioxidants in the plant include catechins, quercetin, and kaempferol.

Hepatoprotective: Maidenhair tree capsules can protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol, which is important for hangover recovery. This property is due to the presence of saponins, which have been shown to have a protective effect on liver cells.

Diuretic: Maidenhair tree capsules have a diuretic effect, which helps eliminate toxins from the body and reduce fluid retention, both of which can alleviate hangover symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of glycosides in the plant.

Nervine: Maidenhair tree capsules have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and irritability associated with hangovers. This property is due to the presence of essential oils, such as bornyl acetate and α-pinene, which have a sedative effect on the nervous system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that help replenish nutrients and rehydrate the body, making them effective for hangover relief.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, leaves help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption.
Seeds: High in essential amino acids and minerals, seeds support liver function and detoxification, aiding in hangover recovery.
Bark: Contains tannins that help constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, alleviating hangover symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsule helps with hangover because it contains parthenolide, a compound that is believed to help reduce the severity of hangover symptoms. Parthenolide is known to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, chemicals that play a role in inflammation and are produced in larger amounts during a hangover. By reducing prostaglandin production, feverfew may help alleviate symptoms such as headache, nausea, and sensitivity to light and sound that are commonly associated with a hangover. Additionally, feverfew is also known for its ability to ease muscle tension and promote relaxation, which can further help to alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew contains parthenolide, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviates hangover-induced headaches and nausea.
Antiemetic: Feverfew's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, aid in reducing hangover-related nausea and vomiting.
Vascular smooth muscle relaxant: Feverfew's ability to relax blood vessels, thanks to parthenolide, helps alleviate hangover-induced tension headaches and migraines.
Antioxidant: Feverfew's antioxidant properties, derived from its flavonoid content, help protect the liver from oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption.
Serotonin modulation: Feverfew's ability to inhibit serotonin release, due to parthenolide, helps regulate mood and alleviate anxiety and depression associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Feverfew leaves are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for hangovers due to their ability to relieve symptoms such as headaches and nausea.

Flowers: The flowers of the feverfew plant are used in medicinal capsules for hangovers because they contain parthenolides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate hangover symptoms.
Seeds: Feverfew seeds are used in medicinal capsules for hangovers as they contain high levels of antioxidants that can help the body recover from the effects of alcohol.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, feverfew stems can still be used in medicinal capsules for hangovers as they contain compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as inflammation and nausea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsule helps with hangover because it contains a powerful compound called thujone, which is known to stimulate the digestive system and promote the metabolism of alcohol in the body. This action helps to reduce the severity of hangover symptoms such as nausea, headache, and fatigue. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to hangover symptoms. Furthermore, wormwood is also believed to have liver-protecting properties, which can help to reduce the damaging effects of alcohol on the liver. All of these factors make wormwood capsule a useful remedy for those looking to prevent or alleviate hangover symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood capsules can reduce inflammation in the body, which is important for hangover as alcohol consumption can cause inflammation in the liver and digestive system. The active constituent thujone is believed to have this anti-inflammatory effect.

Antioxidant: Wormwood contains antioxidants that can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which are produced in larger quantities during alcohol metabolism. This is important for hangover as it can help reduce oxidative stress and cellular damage. The active constituent flavonoids are responsible for wormwood's antioxidant properties.

Digestive stimulant: Wormwood can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which is important for hangover as alcohol can slow down digestion and lead to symptoms such as nausea and bloating. The active constituent absinthin is a bitter compound that stimulates the digestive system.

Anxiolytic: Wormwood has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce anxiety and agitation, common symptoms of a hangover. The active constituent chamazulene is believed to have this anxiolytic effect.
Analgesic: Wormwood has analgesic properties, which can help reduce headaches and muscle pain, common symptoms of a hangover. The active constituent thujone is believed to have this analgesic effect.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the wormwood plant are commonly used in making medicinal capsules for hangovers due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as thujone, which can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover by stimulating digestion and promoting liver function.

Flowers: The flowers of the wormwood plant contain potent essential oils that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making them useful in treating headaches and other symptoms associated with hangovers.

Seeds: The seeds of the wormwood plant are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making them useful in protecting the liver from damage caused by alcohol consumption.

Bark: The bark of the wormwood plant contains high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsule helps with hangover because it contains natural ingredients that work to alleviate the symptoms associated with excessive alcohol consumption. Ginger has been used for centuries as a natural remedy for nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort, all of which are common symptoms of a hangover. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from symptoms like abdominal pain and bloating. Additionally, ginger has been shown to help regulate blood sugar levels, which can become imbalanced after a night of drinking. By taking a ginger capsule before or after drinking, individuals can help prevent or alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, making it an effective and natural solution for those looking to recover from a night of indulgence.

remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for hangovers due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and headaches.
Roots: Ginger roots contain bioactive compounds that help speed up alcohol metabolism and elimination, making them an effective remedy for hangovers.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are rich in essential oils and antioxidants that help protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol consumption, thereby reducing hangover symptoms.
Flowers: Ginger flowers have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and body aches.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsule helps with hangover because it contains curcumin, a potent antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver and brain caused by excessive alcohol consumption. Curcumin also aids in the detoxification process by increasing the production of enzymes responsible for breaking down alcohol, thereby accelerating the elimination of toxins from the body. Moreover, turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with hangovers. Therefore, taking turmeric capsules before or after drinking can help minimize the severity and duration of a hangover.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Curcumin, the active ingredient in turmeric, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which can alleviate hangover symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Antioxidant: Turmeric contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can help the liver metabolize alcohol more efficiently and reduce hangover symptoms.
Hepatoprotective: Turmeric has been shown to protect the liver from damage, which can help the body process and eliminate alcohol more efficiently and reduce the severity of a hangover.
Antiemetic: Curcumin has been shown to have antiemetic properties, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with hangovers.
Analgesic: Turmeric has natural pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate headaches and muscle pain associated with hangovers.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Turmeric's underground stem, or rhizome, contains curcuminoids that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which may alleviate hangover symptoms.
Roots: The turmeric roots, closely related to the rhizomes, have similar medicinal properties and can aid in liver detoxification, potentially helping to process alcohol-related toxins.
Leaves: Turmeric leaves contain essential oils and curcuminoids, which can help protect the liver and reduce inflammation, thus potentially easing hangover symptoms.
Flowers: Turmeric flowers possess curcumin and other bioactive compounds, which can support liver function and help alleviate oxidative stress, potentially reducing hangover severity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsule helps with hangover because it contains silymarin, a flavonoid that supports liver health and function. The liver is responsible for metabolizing alcohol, and excessive consumption can lead to inflammation and damage. Milk thistle extract has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect liver cells from alcohol-induced oxidative stress and support their regeneration. Additionally, milk thistle may help reduce symptoms of hangover such as nausea, headache, and fatigue by regulating glutathione levels, a powerful antioxidant that plays a crucial role in detoxification. Overall, milk thistle capsule can help alleviate hangover symptoms by promoting liver health and function, and supporting the body's natural detoxification processes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its active constituent silymarin, can help reduce inflammation in the liver and alleviate hangover symptoms.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of milk thistle, also attributed to silymarin, help protect liver cells from damage caused by oxidative stress during a hangover.
Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle's hepatoprotective effects, primarily from silymarin, support liver health and function, which is crucial during a hangover as the liver metabolizes alcohol.

Detoxification: Milk thistle supports the detoxification process in the liver by promoting the production of glutathione, a powerful antioxidant that helps eliminate toxins, such as alcohol metabolites.
Immune support: Milk thistle's immune-boosting properties can help strengthen the body's defenses during a hangover, as alcohol consumption can weaken the immune system.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant are the most commonly used part for making medicinal capsules for hangovers due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid that helps the liver to process alcohol and reduce inflammation.

Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are also used in some medicinal preparations for hangovers, as they contain silymarin and other antioxidants that can help to protect the liver from damage caused by alcohol consumption.

Flowers: While not as commonly used as the seeds or leaves, the flowers of the milk thistle plant can also be used in medicinal preparations for hangovers, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to support liver function and reduce symptoms of a hangover.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsule helps with hangover because it contains active compounds that can help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with alcohol consumption. One of the main active ingredients in licorice is glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to help protect the lining of the stomach and reduce inflammation. Additionally, licorice may help to regulate the levels of certain hormones in the body, such as cortisol and aldosterone, which can become imbalanced during a night of heavy drinking. By helping to restore balance to these hormones, licorice may help to reduce symptoms such as headache, nausea, and fatigue. However, it is important to note that licorice should be used in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to serious side effects.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice capsules contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation caused by alcohol consumption, alleviating hangover symptoms.
Adrenal support: Glycyrrhizin and other active compounds support the adrenal glands, helping to regulate stress hormones and counteract the negative effects of alcohol.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice contains flavonoids and chalcones, which have antioxidant effects, helping to neutralize free radicals and protect the liver from alcohol-induced damage.
Gastroprotective effects: Licorice helps soothe the gastrointestinal tract, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by alcohol, and promoting stomach lining healing.
Diuretic properties: Licorice stimulates diuresis, helping the body eliminate toxins and excess fluids, which can reduce hangover symptoms like headaches and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to protect the liver and support alcohol metabolism.
Seeds: Licorice seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat hangovers due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and coumarins, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, making it useful for treating hangovers.
Leaves: Licorice leaves are rich in antioxidants, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by alcohol consumption, making them useful for hangover relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Korean ginseng capsule helps with hangover because it is rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds that support the body's ability to handle stress, including the stress caused by alcohol consumption. Ginseng has been shown to protect the liver, promote alcohol metabolism, and reduce inflammation, all of which can help alleviate hangover symptoms. Additionally, ginseng can help balance blood sugar levels, which can become imbalanced due to alcohol consumption, further contributing to hangover symptoms. By taking a Korean ginseng capsule before or after drinking, individuals may experience reduced severity and duration of hangovers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: This property helps reduce inflammation in the body, including the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms of hangover such as headaches and nausea. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginsenosides and saponins.

Antioxidant: Korean ginseng has antioxidant properties that help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, common symptoms of a hangover. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginsenosides, polyphenols, and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Korean ginseng has been shown to have a protective effect on the liver, helping to reduce damage caused by alcohol consumption. This property can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover by supporting liver function and reducing liver inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginsenosides and compounds such as Rb1 and Rg1.

Adaptogenic: Korean ginseng has adaptogenic properties, which means it helps the body adapt to stress and promotes homeostasis. This property can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover by supporting the body's ability to recover from the stress of alcohol consumption. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginsenosides and eleutherosides.

Diuretic: Korean ginseng has diuretic properties, which can help the body eliminate toxins more efficiently. This property can help alleviate symptoms of a hangover by supporting the body's natural detoxification process and reducing fluid retention. The active constituents responsible for this property include ginsenosides and saponins.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Korean ginseng are the most commonly used part of the plant for medicinal purposes, as they contain high levels of active compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can help to alleviate hangover symptoms.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of Korean ginseng contain many beneficial compounds, including saponins and polysaccharides, which can help to support the immune system and improve liver function, making them useful for hangover relief.

Seeds: The seeds of Korean ginseng contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the symptoms of a hangover.

Leaves: While not as commonly used as the roots or rhizomes, the leaves of Korean ginseng do contain some beneficial compounds, including flavonoids and triterpenoids, which can help to protect the liver and support the immune system.

Flowers: The flowers of Korean ginseng are less commonly used in medicinal applications, but they do contain some beneficial compounds, including flavonoids and volatile oils, which can help to support the immune system and reduce inflammation.

The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects can also help to protect the liver and reduce oxidative stress caused by alcohol consumption. Furthermore, ashwagandha can help to improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted during a hangover. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, ashwagandha can help to alleviate some of the symptoms associated with a hangover, such as headache, nausea, and fatigue.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of withanolides, can help reduce inflammation in the body caused by alcohol consumption.
Adaptogenic: As an adaptogen, ashwagandha helps the body manage stress, including the stress caused by a hangover, by regulating the immune and nervous systems.

Nervine: The nervine properties of ashwagandha, due to the presence of alkaloids and withanolides, can help soothe and calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and irritability associated with hangovers.
Antioxidant: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties, due to the presence of withanolides, can help reduce oxidative stress and free radical damage caused by alcohol consumption.
Diuretic: Ashwagandha's diuretic properties can help eliminate toxins from the body, including those caused by alcohol consumption, by increasing urine production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ashwagandha plant are commonly used to make medicinal capsules for hangovers due to their high concentration of withanolides, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and stress in the body.

Leaves: The leaves of the ashwagandha plant are used in medicinal capsules for hangovers because they contain adaptogenic properties that can help to balance the body's systems and promote overall well-being.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hangover with herbal capsules is by using those made from natural ingredients known to detoxify and rehydrate the body. Look for supplements containing milk thistle, ginger, and B-vitamins, which help protect the liver, alleviate nausea, and restore essential nutrients. Always follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hangover that are treated with herbal capsules include nausea, dehydration, and fatigue. Certain herbs, such as milk thistle, ginger, and turmeric, can help alleviate symptoms by supporting liver function, reducing inflammation, and soothing the stomach. These herbs can be found in various herbal supplements and capsules, providing a natural and holistic approach to treating hangover-like symptoms. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.
